slide.caption: 🎬 Lights, Camera, Trivia! — How well do you know the biggest movies ever made?

items.text: In Jurassic Park (1993), what is the first dinosaur Dr. Grant and Dr. Sattler see alive?
What is the name of the fictional African nation in Marvel's Black Panther?
Who voiced the villain Scar in Disney's The Lion King (1994)?
In Inception, what object does Cobb use as his totem to check if he's dreaming?
How many Academy Awards did The Return of the King win?
Who directed the 1975 thriller Jaws?
In The Matrix, what color pill does Morpheus offer Neo to escape the simulation?
What is the name of the AI defense network in the Terminator franchise?
What speed must the DeLorean reach to time travel in Back to the Future?
What is Val Kilmer's call sign in Top Gun (1986)?

slide.caption: 📼 Rewind to the '90s! — Dial-up modems, boy bands, and frosted tips.

items.text: Which company launched the Tamagotchi virtual pet in 1996?
Which grunge band released Smells Like Teen Spirit in 1991?
What hit sitcom was set in a coffee shop called Central Perk?
Which toy triggered a collectibles craze with pellet-filled plush animals in the late '90s?
Michael Jordan starred alongside Bugs Bunny in which 1996 film?
Which pop group launched the 'Girl Power' movement with their 1996 hit Wannabe?
Which studio released Toy Story, the world's first fully computer-animated feature film, in 1995?
What internet service was famous for mailing CDs and saying 'You've Got Mail!'?
What was the first 32-bit home console Sony released in North America?
Which iMac G3 feature made it stand out from every other computer in 1998?

slide.caption: ☕ No hugging, no learning — just Seinfeld trivia. Good luck, Newman.

items.text: What is the name of George Costanza's recurring fake job at the Yankees?
What is Kramer's first name, revealed in the finale?
What does George order at Monk's Café that becomes his ultimate comfort food?
What is the name of Jerry's annoying neighbor across the hall?
What is Elaine's last name?
Which episode features the gang waiting for a table at a Chinese restaurant for the entire episode?
What candy bar does George try to eat with a knife and fork?
What does George's answering machine message say he is?
What city is Elaine's boss J. Peterman based out of for most of the series?
What was the name of Jerry and George's failed TV pilot?

slide.caption: 🔬 Science time! — Cells, atoms, ecosystems, and everything in between.

items.text: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
What is the chemical symbol for water?
Which planet is closest to the Sun?
What force pulls objects toward the center of the Earth?
What do plants use to make food from sunlight?
What is the smallest unit of an element that still has its chemical properties?
What type of rock is formed from cooled lava or magma?
How many bones are in the adult human body?
What gas do plants absorb from the air during photosynthesis?
What is the speed of light in a vacuum (approximately)?

slide.caption: 🎸 Turn it up! — From vinyl to streaming, test your music knowledge.

items.text: Which artist released Thriller in 1982, the best-selling album of all time?
What Fender guitar model launched in 1954 became iconic for rock and blues?
Which band recorded The Dark Side of the Moon at Abbey Road Studios?
Hip Hop culture was born at a 1973 block party hosted by DJ Kool Herc in which borough?
Who was the lead vocalist of Queen until his passing in 1991?
Which jazz vocalist was known as the 'First Lady of Song'?
What Detroit record label pioneered the crossover pop-soul sound in the 1960s?
Bob Dylan shocked fans at which 1965 festival by going electric?
Which drum machine released in 1980 defined the sound of hip hop and electronic music?
What landmark music event took place on Max Yasgur's farm in Bethel, New York in 1969?

slide.caption: 🏆 Game On! — From the Olympics to the World Cup, how deep does your sports knowledge go?

items.text: Which country won the very first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
Who holds the men's 100m world record with a time of 9.58 seconds?
How far is a regulation marathon?
Which tennis player holds the most Grand Slam singles titles in the Open Era?
What color jersey does the overall leader wear at the Tour de France?
Who achieved the first perfect 10.0 in Olympic gymnastics at the 1976 Montreal Games?
Which country has won the most all-time Winter Olympic medals?
Who held the MLB career home run record of 714 before Hank Aaron?
How many players from one team are on the ice at once in standard NHL play (including goalie)?
Which sport uses the terms Birdie, Bogey, and Eagle?

slide.caption: 🚀 3... 2... 1... Lift off! — Test your knowledge of the cosmos.

items.text: How long does it take light from the Sun to reach Earth?
Which planet has the most visible ring system?
What is the boundary around a black hole where nothing can escape?
Which space telescope was launched into orbit in 1990?
Pluto was reclassified from a planet to what in 2006?
Which NASA program first landed humans on the Moon in 1969?
Which moon of Jupiter may have a liquid ocean beneath its icy surface?
Which probe was the first human-made object to reach interstellar space?
What is the largest volcano in our solar system?
What is the closest major spiral galaxy to the Milky Way?

slide.caption: 🌍 Around the World! — From mountain peaks to ocean trenches.

items.text: Which country spans the most time zones in the world?
Which is the largest island in the world that isn't its own continent?
What is the capital of Australia — chosen as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne?
Which mountain range contains Mount Everest?
What is the deepest known point on Earth?
What canal opened in 1869 connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea?
Which country is nicknamed the 'Land of Fire and Ice'?
Which South American country has coastline on both the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea?
Which high-altitude plateau is nicknamed the 'Roof of the World'?
Which African lake feeds the White Nile?

slide.caption: 🌴 Welcome to the Sunshine State! — From Space Coast to South Beach, how well do you know Florida?

items.text: What is the capital city of Florida?
Which Florida city is home to NASA's Kennedy Space Center?
What is the name of Florida's famous freshwater ecosystem covering much of the southern peninsula?
Which Florida city is known as the theme park capital of the world?
What is the largest lake entirely within Florida?
Florida is the southernmost contiguous U.S. state. What city is its southernmost point?
Which NFL team plays its home games in Miami?
What citrus fruit is most closely associated with Florida's agricultural identity?
The Florida Keys are connected to the mainland by which famous highway?
What prehistoric reptile, native to Florida, was once hunted nearly to extinction before federal protection?

slide.caption: 💻 Byte-sized brilliance — From punch cards to AI, how well do you know tech history?

items.text: Which British pioneer is credited with conceptualizing the first general-purpose computer in the 1820s?
What year did Tim Berners-Lee publicly release the World Wide Web?
Which open-source operating system kernel was uploaded by Finnish student Linus Torvalds in 1991?
Which early internet network, commissioned by the U.S. Department of Defense in 1969, laid the foundation for the modern internet?
Which object-oriented programming language was deployed commercially by Sun Microsystems in 1995?
Which consumer electronics company launched the Walkman TPS-L2 in the summer of 1979?
What wireless protocol standard was named after 10th-century Danish king Harald Gormsson?
What does CPU stand for?
Which storage medium, invented by IBM in 1971, used a magnetic recording sheet inside a flexible sleeve?
GPS requires at least how many satellites in its constellation to function?

slide.caption: 🍳 You are what you eat — but do you know what you're eating? Let's find out.

items.text: What sauce is the base for classic Eggs Benedict?
What is the most expensive spice in the world by weight?
What chemical process causes the browning of seared meat when amino acids and sugars react under heat?
Which country is the historical birthplace of modern pizza?
What is the Japanese cooking method that vacuum-seals food in plastic and cooks it at precise low water temperatures?
What scale is used to measure the spiciness of chili peppers?
What gives traditional Italian pasta dough its elastic, chewy texture?
Which Korean fermented dish is made from napa cabbage layered with chili pepper paste?
What type of pastry is created by repeatedly folding cold butter into dough to create hundreds of flaky layers?
To be legally stamped Parmigiano-Reggiano, the cheese must be made from what?
options: Raw unpasteurized cow's milk aged 12+ months
Sheep's milk aged in limestone caves

slide.caption: 👋 Quick Pulse Check — No wrong answers here. Let's hear from everyone.

items.text: How are you feeling coming into today's session?
What best describes your workload right now?
options: Manageable — I'm on top of things

items.text: How clear is your team on current priorities?
options: Very clear — we know exactly what matters

items.text: How connected do you feel to the rest of the team?
When do you do your best focused work?
What's the biggest obstacle to your productivity right now?
How often does your team celebrate wins — big or small?
options: Regularly, it's part of our culture

items.text: Which working style fits you best?

slide.caption: 📋 Help us plan the perfect event — Your input shapes what comes next.

items.text: What type of event format do you prefer?
What day of the week works best for you?
What time slot is most convenient?
What kind of food setup do you prefer at events?
Which session format do you get the most value from?
How much networking time should be built in?
What makes you most likely to attend an event?

slide.caption: 🎯 All-Hands Kickoff — Polls, Q&A, a rating, and a word cloud. Something for everyone.

items.text: How long have you been with the company?
Which department are you part of?
What's the one thing you're most excited about for the year ahead?
What's your biggest challenge heading into this quarter?
How confident are you in the company's direction for this year?
In one or two words — what does success look like to YOU this year?
What communication channel does your team rely on most?
What's one thing leadership could do to better support your team?

slide.caption: 📚 Knowledge Check — Let's see what landed from the session so far.

items.text: Before today, how familiar were you with this product?
options: Heard of it but never tried

items.text: Which feature are you most excited to explore?
Which question type is best for gathering open-ended feedback?
What does 'Hidden Reveal' mode mean in Tally ARS?
options: Results stay hidden until the host reveals them
The question is invisible to attendees
Only the host can see the answers
Results are deleted after the session

items.text: How intuitive did you find the product to set up and use?
What's one use case from your own work where you'd use this?
What word best describes the Tally ARS experience so far?
How likely are you to use this at your next event or meeting?
options: Maybe, need to think about it
Probably not for my use case

slide.caption: 🗣️ AAC App Research — Help us build the next generation of communication tools. Your experience matters.

items.text: What is your primary relationship to AAC?
options: Parent / caregiver of an AAC user

items.text: Which AAC app does your child or client use most?
What is your biggest frustration with current AAC apps?
How much does your family or organization currently pay annually for AAC software?
Which AI-powered feature would excite you most in a next-gen AAC app?
options: AI that learns vocabulary from the user's patterns
Voice that sounds natural and personalized
AI-generated situation phrases in real time

items.text: How important is offline functionality — no internet required — to you?
How satisfied are you with the AAC tools currently available on the market?
What platform does your AAC user primarily use?
What's one thing you wish every AAC app developer understood about your daily reality?
In one or two words — what does a great AAC app feel like to you?

slide.caption: ☁️ Word Cloud Demo — Watch the crowd's voice take shape in real time.

items.text: What's the first word that comes to mind when you think about great leadership?
Name one word that describes how you feel about the future of AI.
What's the most important quality in a great teammate?
One word: what does innovation mean to you?
What's one word that captures the culture of this organization?
Name a skill you want to build or improve in the next 12 months.
What word best describes the energy in the room right now?

slide.caption: ⭐ Rating Scale Demo — From 1 to 10, tell us how you really feel.

items.text: How satisfied are you with how today's event has been organized so far?
How would you rate the clarity of communication from leadership this quarter?
How likely are you to recommend this organization as a great place to work?
How energized do you feel about the quarter ahead?
How well does your team handle conflict and disagreement constructively?
items.labelHigh: Very well — we work through it

items.text: How confident are you in the tools and resources you have to do your job well?
Overall, how valuable was today's session for you?

slide.caption: 💬 Open Q&A — Your turn to speak. Responses appear live for the whole room.

items.text: What's the most valuable thing you learned or heard today?
What's a question you still have that wasn't answered today?
What's one thing you'll do differently as a result of today's session?
What's the biggest challenge your team is facing right now?
If you could change one thing about how we work together, what would it be?
What's a win from the past 30 days you're proud of — big or small?
What's your biggest hope for this team over the next 6 months?

decks.name: Ice Breaker — Get to Know the Room

slide.caption: 🧊 Break the Ice! — Fun, fast questions to warm up the room before we get into it.

items.text: How do you take your coffee or morning drink?
What's your working setup like most days?
Pick your spirit animal for this week.
If your work style was a movie genre, what would it be?
options: Comedy — keep it light and fun
Thriller — deadline crunches and plot twists

items.text: In one word — what did you do last weekend?
What's your preferred way to unwind after a long day?
options: Get outside for a walk or workout
